  • Source of Truth: Resolution will use the official Israeli military statement about the strike, as published on official channels or quoted consistently by major news organizations on or by 20 February 2026.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when the Israeli military publicly identifies the organization it says operated from the struck command centre, expected by 20 February 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the single English name of the armed group or organization named by the Israeli military, in 1-3 words.

South Africa is expected to name a squad for a five-match Twenty20 international tour in March. The tour captain will be announced with the squad or before the first match. criteria:
  • Cricket South Africa's official squad announcement for the March 2026 T20 tour of New Zealand, or if needed, official CSA match materials for the first game of that tour.

  • The full name of the player officially named as captain for the tour.

Border tensions between Pakistan and Afghanistan sometimes lead to competing official claims about where military force was used. If Afghan authorities accuse Pakistan of air raids before the resolution date, this question asks which province names they will publicly identify. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official statements from Afghanistan's Ministry of Defence, Ministry of Foreign Affairs, or other Afghan government spokespersons, or consistent reporting from major news outlets quoting those statements.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on or before February 23, 2026, when Afghan officials publicly name the provinces affected in the relevant raids.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must list all province names explicitly identified in the resolving source, in the same order they are presented there.
Q 59953 Off the coast of which Greek island will authorities report that a migrant boat capsized by 22 February 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-14. Migrant and asylum-seeker crossings in the Mediterranean continue, and Greek rescue services sometimes respond to boat emergencies near the country's islands. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official statements from the Hellenic Coast Guard or other Greek authorities, supplemented if needed by Reuters, AFP, or the Athens News Agency.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on the date, by 22 February 2026, when the relevant authorities or widely cited wire-service reports identify the Greek island off whose coast the migrant boat capsized.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the island name only, using the common English spelling as reported by the resolving source.
Q 109135 By February 28, 2026, which Ukrainian city will be reported as the site of a deadly attack in which two explosions hit police responding to an emergency at a store? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-01. Ukraine has many cities, and major public-safety incidents can happen in different places. News reports and local authorities usually identify the city where a serious incident takes place. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: This question will resolve based on official statements from Ukrainian authorities and consistent reporting by major news organizations published no later than February 28, 2026 that clearly name the city where the incident occurred.
  • Resolution Date: The resolution occurs on the earliest date by February 28, 2026 when authoritative reporting clearly identifies the city.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the English name of the city only, using standard spelling.
Q 162065 Which country will finish the 2026 Winter Olympics with 30 total medals, setting a new national Winter Olympics record, by 22 February 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-19. The 2026 Winter Olympics are scheduled for February 2026. Final medal standings become official after the last events are completed and the Games close. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: The official Milano Cortina 2026 or IOC final medal table, supported if needed by official Olympics results books or major wire-service reports published after the last medal events.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on 22 February 2026, once the final medal table for the 2026 Winter Olympics is officially published.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Country name only, in English, matching the nation credited with exactly a specific number of total medals that sets a new national Winter Olympics record.

  • The answer must be the alliance’s uppercase acronym or letter-based short name exactly as publicly reported.
Q 493481 Which country’s government will say it “strongly condemns” Pakistan’s air strikes on Afghan territory by February 27, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-09. Cross-border air strikes in South Asia can draw public responses from nearby governments, especially when civilian harm is alleged. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: An official government or foreign ministry statement, or remarks by an official spokesperson, confirmed by the government’s own channels and/or major news organizations such as Reuters, AP, AFP, or equivalent outlets.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on the first date on or before 27th February 2026 when a government is publicly reported using the phrase “strongly condemns” in response to Pakistan’s air strikes on Afghan territory.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the country name in English.
Q 73075 Which ambassador will France say it is summoning over comments on Quentin Deranque’s death by February 28, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-12. A deadly political assault in France is drawing national attention and could lead to a formal diplomatic response if foreign officials make public statements about it. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official statements from France’s Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs, the relevant embassy, or consistent reporting by major outlets such as Reuters, AFP, AP, Le Monde, France Inter, or France Info.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on the first date by February 28, 2026 when France publicly identifies the ambassador it says it will summon in connection with comments about Quentin Deranque’s death.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The full personal name of the ambassador, written as given name followed by surname in standard Latin script.
Q 992626 Which team will be in first place in Group 1 of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup Super Eights by the end of February 25, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-14. The Super Eights round of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup uses a group table based on match results. One team will occupy first place in Group 1 by the close of play on February 25, 2026. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: The official ICC Group 1 Super Eights standings published on February 25, 2026. If teams are level on points, the team shown in first place by ICC at the time of resolution will be used.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves at the end of February 25, 2026, based on the latest official ICC Group 1 table for that date.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the official team name listed in first place on the ICC table.
Q 648994 Which organization will be identified as the owner of the Thai ammunition site struck by explosions by the end of February 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-09. Thailand's internal security system includes several police and security bodies that operate facilities around the country. This question asks which organization will later be named as owning the site involved in a reported explosion incident. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: This question will resolve using consistent reporting from major news outlets published on or before 28th February 2026 and any official statements from Thai authorities identifying the owning or operating body.
  • Resolution Date: The question resolves when reliable public reporting clearly names the organization associated with the site, no later than 28th February 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the official English name of the organization as reported in the resolving source. Country possessives should be omitted unless they are part of the formal name.
Q 654360 Which state in southeastern Brazil will report at least 23 deaths from torrential-rain flooding by February 24, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-16. Southeastern Brazil is in its rainy season, and severe rainfall can trigger floods, landslides, and emergency rescue operations. State and local authorities may issue casualty updates as conditions develop. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official updates from Brazilian state or civil defense authorities, supported by reporting from major news agencies such as Reuters, AFP, or AP.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on the first credible report, no later than 24th February 2026, that identifies the southeastern Brazilian state where the flood death toll has reached at least 23.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the full name of the Brazilian state, written as a short text string.
Q 392014 Which city will Congolese authorities say had two mass graves discovered on its outskirts by February 27, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-31. Fighting continues in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, and reports of attacks on civilians may emerge from major towns in the Kivu region. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Public statements from Congolese provincial authorities or reporting from a major international news agency published by February 27, 2026.
  • Resolution Date: The question resolves on the first public report or statement identifying the city, no later than February 27, 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: A single city name in English.
Q 632535 Which team will Spain announce as its final warm-up opponent before the 2026 World Cup by March 27, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-23. Spain is finalizing its pre-World Cup schedule, including one last exhibition match before the tournament begins. National federations often confirm these fixtures only shortly before the competition. criteria:
  • This question resolves using an official announcement or schedule update from the RFEF. Federation-backed fixture lists or major wire reports may be used as secondary confirmation.
  • The question resolves when Spain officially names its final pre-World Cup warm-up opponent, no later than 27th March 2026.
  • Provide the official short country name of the announced opponent, written as a 1-3 word team name.
Q 726747 In which UK city will police report arresting a man after he allegedly enters a mosque with an axe and other weapons during evening tarawih prayers, by February 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-09. During Ramadan, mosques in the United Kingdom hold evening tarawih prayers that can draw large congregations. Serious security incidents at places of worship are usually documented by police statements and major news outlets. criteria:
  • The question resolves using the first official police statement, mosque statement, or multiple consistent reports from major UK news organizations describing the incident by 29th February 2026.
  • The resolution occurs on the date the arrest and incident are first publicly confirmed, no later than 29th February 2026.
  • Provide the city name where the mosque is located. The answer should be a single city name.
Q 415069 Which country will host the next round of indirect US-Iran talks on Monday, March 2, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-19. The United States and Iran are engaged in indirect negotiations over Iran's nuclear programme, and different rounds may be held in different countries. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: official announcements from the governments involved in the talks or the mediator, with major wire services such as Reuters or AP as backup
  • Resolution Date: this question resolves when the venue of the Monday round is officially named or the meeting begins, no later than 3rd March 2026
  • Accepted Answer Format: the English name of the host country
Q 489902 In which city will the next technical talks between Iranian and US officials be held in early March 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-19. Iran and the United States are holding indirect diplomatic talks through mediators over nuclear and regional security issues. A further technical meeting is expected soon after the current round. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Resolve using the first public confirmation from Iran’s Foreign Ministry, Oman’s Foreign Ministry, or consistent reporting by major international news agencies that identifies the location of the next technical US-Iran talks.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves no later than 6 March 2026, when the location of that next technical meeting is publicly confirmed.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the city name only, in English.
Q 443306 Where will Donald Trump say the final talks before the joint US-Israeli strikes had collapsed by the end of March 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-19. Diplomatic talks over Iran’s nuclear programme are continuing in international venues. If negotiations fail, later statements may identify the city where the last round broke down. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: A public statement or interview from Donald Trump, supported by transcripts or reporting from major outlets such as Reuters, AP, or The New York Post, by March 31, 2026.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when the city of the collapsed final talks is clearly identified in public reporting, no later than March 31, 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the city name only.
Q 333107 Who will South Africa play in the first ICC Men's T20 World Cup semifinal on Wednesday, 4 March 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-02-18. The ICC Men's T20 World Cup is in its Super Eight stage, and the semifinal matchups will be fixed once the final qualification results are known. South Africa is among the teams still competing for knockout placement. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: The ICC's official Men's T20 World Cup fixtures page, official semifinal schedule, or official pre-match materials published by the ICC.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when South Africa's semifinal opponent is officially listed, no later than the scheduled start of that semifinal on 4 March 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The answer must be the full team name of South Africa's opponent.

- Deranque city, Navalny toxin, and Bologna-Venice rail section: raised confidence on the already-strong linked evidence as their resolution dates approach.
- Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district: a Feb. 11 Khaleej Times article places a fresh police attack on the outskirts of Dera Ismail Khan, near Waziristan. Added Dera Ismail Khan as lead for the northwest Pakistan police-station bomb question, while keeping Bannu and North Waziristan tails.
- T20 markets: Pakistan remains top of Group A after beating the USA, while Australia are missing Marsh/Tim David plus the Cummins-Hazlewood-Starc pace core. Raised Australia failure risk, added Ireland as a live alternate for Pakistan's Super Eights opener, and broadened Super Eights Group 1 leader to include Pakistan and South Africa.
- US-Iran talks venues: Larijani travelled from Oman to Qatar, and reporting emphasized Qatar's role as a past mediator. Trimmed Oman/Muscat slightly and raised Qatar/Doha as the main alternate.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market strike town, Bangladesh cabinet woman, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Democratic SOTU response, Dutch AI-tool case, Iranian frigate, Lebanon command-centre group, South Africa T20 captain, France-US repost source and Winter Olympics medal/hockey questions had no Feb. 11 signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-12 Update

Resolution events:
- Bangladesh main opposition resolved to Jamaat-e-Islami, matching the lead. The BNP-win/Jamaat-opposition read was correct.
- Second Middle East carrier resolved to Gerald R Ford. I kept Ford only as a tail and overweighted George H.W. Bush/George Washington after the Reuters candidate list. Lesson: when an already-deployed carrier is named as a possible option from a nearby theater, keep more mass on the operationally available ship even if press says other candidates are "most likely".
- Deranque city resolved to Lyon, matching the high-confidence linked-event read.

Updates made:
- Dutch AI-tool court case: older UK/French/Indonesian reporting and Feb. 12 xAI coverage keep pointing to Grok as the image tool under explicit/deepfake scrutiny. Raised Grok and trimmed generic image-model alternatives.
- Bangladesh cabinet woman: election-day reporting says the BNP is the frontrunner, Jamaat is its main rival, and Jamaat fielded no women candidates. Added BNP women Rumeen Farhana, Selima Rahman and Shama Obaid while retaining Sharmeen S Murshid as a technocratic/interim-continuity possibility.
- T20 markets: India beat Namibia by 93 runs and now top Group A on net run rate, Sri Lanka crushed Oman for a second win, and England's loss to West Indies leaves them needing two wins. Shifted the former-champion failure risk toward Australia/England and away from Sri Lanka; made Sri Lanka the slight lead for Pakistan's Super Eights opener; added West Indies as a live Super Eights Group 1 leader candidate.
- Pakistan clinching opponent: India and Pakistan both have two wins, but Namibia are 0-2 after losses to the Netherlands and India. Kept Namibia as the lead final-clincher, cut the stale USA mass, and added a modest India tail in case Pakistan clinches by beating India.
- Navalny toxin and Olympic rail: with no contrary Feb. 12 signal and direct prior reporting still intact, raised Novichok and Bologna-Venice slightly before their near-term resolution dates.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market strike town, northwest Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group and South Africa T20 captain had no Feb. 12 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-13 Update

Resolution events:
- Navalny toxin resolved to epibatidine. I missed badly by anchoring on the historical Novichok poisoning. Lesson: synthetic-event questions can deliberately use a plausible but non-obvious toxin; leave tail mass for unfamiliar chemicals when wording says "new scientific findings".
- Olympic rail section resolved to Rome and Naples. I missed by over-weighting the first Reuters/Straits Times Bologna-Venice sabotage wording. Lesson: when a question asks about a later investigated cable-burning section, separate the broad disruption story from the exact resolving incident; do not assume the first geography is the final section.

Updates made:
- T20 former-champion failure: Zimbabwe beat Australia by 23 runs and stayed unbeaten, putting Australia in real danger in Group B. Raised Australia sharply, kept England as the second risk after its West Indies loss, and cut Sri Lanka after its 2-0 start.
- Pakistan Super Eights opener: with Sri Lanka and Zimbabwe now the live Group B leaders and Pakistan likely to remain behind India if the India-Pakistan game is rain-affected, added Zimbabwe and lowered Australia.
- Pakistan clinching opponent: Khaleej Times reported a 93% rain chance for the India-Pakistan match in Colombo. A no-result would push Pakistan's likely clinching win back to Namibia, so raised Namibia and cut India/USA.
- Bangladesh cabinet woman: Al Jazeera and Hindustan Times reported BNP's majority and named seven elected women, including Shama Obaid Islam and Rumeen Farhana. Shifted to elected/full-name candidates and removed stale interim/Awami-era names.
- Dutch AI-tool case: Brazil ordered X to block Grok sexualised deepfakes, adding to Indonesia/UK/France pressure on the same product. Raised Grok again.
- Northwest Pakistan police-station bomb: Reuters/Straits Times again tied a fresh police attack to Dera Ismail Khan on the edge of Waziristan. Raised Dera Ismail Khan.
- Winter Olympics 30-medal country: Independent medal-table coverage says the USA trail hosts Italy and Norway. Since 30 total medals would be a new Italian Winter record, raised Italy materially.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market drone-strike town, Ronda Rousey opponent, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group, South Africa T20 captain, Greek migrant-boat island and US-Iran venue questions had no Feb. 13 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-14 Update

Resolution events:
- No new Feb. 14 resolution events were present; resolved count remained at 156.

Updates made:
- T20 former-champion failure: England beat Scotland and only need to beat Italy to all but secure the Super Eights, so trimmed England's failure risk. Australia remains the main former champion at risk after the Zimbabwe loss and Ireland's big NRR-boosting win over Oman.
- Pakistan Super Eights opener: Pakistan are still likeliest to finish behind India in Group A, while Sri Lanka and Zimbabwe are the live Group B leaders. Raised Zimbabwe close to Sri Lanka and lowered Australia.
- Pakistan clinching opponent: reporting says the India-Pakistan winner qualifies immediately, while a rain washout would put both teams through on five points. Reduced the overconfident Namibia forecast and raised India as the direct-clinch alternate if Pakistan win the marquee match.
- Super Eights Group 1 leader: South Africa beat New Zealand convincingly for a third straight Group D win, so raised South Africa while keeping India as the lead.
- South Africa T20 captain: Al Jazeera explicitly described Aiden Markram as captain while he led South Africa over New Zealand. Raised Markram materially.
- Men's Olympic ice hockey: Canada and the United States remain unbeaten and strong; Canada beat Switzerland with McDavid producing, the US beat Denmark, Finland rebounded by beating Sweden and Slovakia won Group B. Raised Canada/Finland/Slovakia and trimmed Sweden.
- US-Iran talks: Switzerland/AFP said Oman would host US-Iran talks in Geneva next week, and Reuters said US envoys would meet Iranians in Geneva with Oman mediating. Raised Oman for host country, moved the technical-talks city lead to Geneva, and shifted the final-collapse venue toward Geneva/Muscat split.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market drone-strike town, northwest Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district, Bangladesh cabinet woman, Ronda Rousey opponent, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group, Greek migrant-boat island and Winter Olympics 30-medal country had no Feb. 14 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-15 Update

Resolution events:
- Pakistan's clinching opponent resolved to Namibia, matching the lead. Good read: the final weak Group A opponent was the cleaner path after the India-Pakistan uncertainty.
- Northwest Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district resolved to Bannu. I had Dera Ismail Khan ahead and Bannu only as a tail. Lesson: for KP police-station attack questions, keep Bannu/Waziristan-area districts substantial even when a recent article names Dera Ismail Khan.
- Sudan crowded-market drone-strike town resolved to Sodari. I missed by leaning on larger central Sudan towns. Lesson: exact-town Sudan strike questions can resolve to smaller/less obvious towns; avoid overconcentrating on state capitals and headline cities without a direct article.

Updates made:
- T20 former-champion failure: India and West Indies are now qualified for the Super Eights, and Pakistan's Namibia clinch is resolved. Raised Australia as the main former-champion failure risk after the Zimbabwe loss, kept England as the main alternate because they still need to beat Italy, and cut Pakistan/West Indies to tiny tails.
- Pakistan Super Eights opener: Pakistan are effectively A2 behind India, so their Feb. 21 opener is likely tied to the Group B leader. Sri Lanka and Zimbabwe remain the live leaders, with Australia reduced after the Zimbabwe defeat.
- Super Eights Group 1 leader: India's qualification and unbeaten Group A position strengthen India as lead. West Indies also qualified and look live after three straight wins; South Africa remains a serious alternate.
- US-Iran talks venues: Feb. 15 reporting says Araghchi left for Geneva with a diplomatic and technical delegation, with Oman mediating/hosting the talks in Geneva. Raised Oman for host country, Geneva for technical-talk city, and Geneva as the likely final-collapse venue if the talks fail.
- Men's Olympic ice hockey: Slovakia won Group B on goal differential and earned a quarter-final bye despite losing to Sweden. Raised Slovakia modestly while keeping Canada and the United States as the gold favorites.

Watched but no update:
- Bangladesh cabinet woman, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Ronda Rousey opponent, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Benfica racist-remark player, Russian military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, Dutch AI-tool case, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group, South Africa T20 captain, Greek migrant-boat island and Winter Olympics 30-medal country had no Feb. 15 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-16 Update

Resolution events:
- Israel bobsleigh broadcaster resolved to RTS. I missed by anchoring on big English-language Olympic broadcasters. Lesson: controversy around Israel at a European Winter Olympics can resolve to a local/regional European broadcaster; include RTS/SRF-style national broadcasters when the event is in continental Europe.
- Bangladesh cabinet woman resolved to Afroza Khanam Rita. I had her but too low behind Shama Obaid Islam and Rumeen Farhana. Lesson: elected and senior BNP women with administrative/regional experience deserve more weight than nationally visible commentators when forecasting full cabinet posts.
- Former T20 champion failing to reach the Super Eight resolved to Australia, matching the raised Feb. 15 lead after the Zimbabwe loss and Sri Lanka fixture risk.
- Ronda Rousey's MMA return opponent resolved to Gina Carano. I missed by focusing on active fighters/rematch logic. Lesson: celebrity/comeback fight markets can favor spectacle and cross-media names over current rankings.
- Western Europe storm resolved to Storm Pedro. I missed by overfitting to prior storm-name pattern guesses. Lesson: storm-name questions need wider name tails unless a meteorological agency has already named the system.
- Russian military-police explosion town resolved to Sertolovo. I missed by using better-known towns near St Petersburg. Lesson: for "near St Petersburg" military-site questions, include garrison/suburban towns, not just large satellite cities.
- Benfica player accused by Vinicius Jr resolved to Gianluca Prestianni. I missed by focusing on senior/defensive Benfica players. Lesson: football incident markets can resolve to younger or less prominent squad players; spread some mass beyond obvious veterans.

Updates made:
- Pakistan Super Eights opener: Hindustan Times explained the pre-seeded Super Eight structure and listed Y3 as Pakistan and Y4 as Sri Lanka. With Pakistan's qualification path resolved and Sri Lanka through, raised Sri Lanka heavily for Pakistan's Feb. 21 opener.
- Super Eights Group 1 leader and South Africa semifinal opponent: the same pre-seeding article placed India with South Africa and West Indies in Group X while Pakistan/Sri Lanka/England/New Zealand sit in Group Y. Raised India for the Group 1 lead, added Zimbabwe as the Australia replacement tail, and shifted South Africa's likely semifinal opponent away from Australia/India toward the Group Y teams.
- Men's Olympic ice hockey: Canada beat France 10-2, earned top preliminary seeding, and Reuters described Canada as the hot favourite. Raised Canada and trimmed Sweden after Sweden fell into a playoff path.
- Winter Olympics 30-medal country: Italy reached 22 medals and a national Winter Games record with several days left. Raised Italy materially for finishing exactly 30 total medals.
- Iranian frigate: with the question newly active and no direct ship report, broadened from only Dena/Sahand to include Jamaran, Alborz and Sabalan.
- Brazil flooding state: no direct Feb. 16 flood report, but the newly active state question needed better coverage across southeastern Brazil, so added Minas Gerais and Espirito Santo behind Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paulo.
- Democratic SOTU response: Wes Moore had a CBS town hall and multiple Feb. 16 stories as a rising Democratic voice; added him behind Gretchen Whitmer and kept Jeffries/Beshear live.
- Deranque/France-US row: the active France-US source question implies the ambassador summoned over Deranque comments is more likely the U.S. ambassador. Raised Charles Kushner and shifted the source-office forecast from "US Embassy in France" toward Washington offices, especially DRL and the State Department spokesperson operation.
- Dutch AI tool: UK/EU coverage still centers Grok in safety and sexualized/deepfake concerns. Raised Grok slightly.
- Lebanon command-centre group: Al Jazeera reported an Israeli strike in Lebanon targeting Palestinian Islamic Jihad members. Hezbollah remains the lead for a command-centre strike, but PIJ is now a meaningful alternate.
- US-Iran talks: Feb. 16 reports said Araghchi was in Geneva and talks were starting there. Raised Geneva further for the technical-talk city and final-collapse venue.

Watched but no update:
- South Africa T20 captain, Afghan air-raid provinces, Greek migrant-boat island, France-US repost article itself, Thai ammunition-site owner, Brazil flooding direct reports, Barcelona midfielder injury, prediction-market Senate bills, UK by-election, Newcastle Champions League opponent and the longer-tail Iran conflict exact-location questions had no Feb. 16 signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-17 Update

Resolution events:
- Pakistan's T20 Super Eights opener resolved to New Zealand. I missed badly after reading the pre-seed labels as implying a Sri Lanka opener. Lesson: for tournament fixtures, do not infer from seed labels alone; map the actual dated fixture slot and opponent group.
- Iranian frigate resolved to IRIS Dena. I had Dena as the lead but lost credit because the accepted exact name included the official prefix. Lesson: when resolution criteria says include official prefix if reported, submit both prefix and no-prefix variants if there is room, or use the prefixed naval style for the lead.

Updates made:
- Dutch AI tool: Ireland's DPC opened a formal investigation into X/Grok over nonconsensual sexualized images, with Reuters/AP-style coverage echoed by several outlets. Raised Grok to very high confidence.
- Lebanon command-centre group: Feb. 17 coverage focused on Lebanon's plan to dismantle Hezbollah's arsenal under US/Israeli pressure. Moved Hezbollah back up and trimmed the Palestinian Islamic Jihad alternate from the prior PIJ vehicle-strike signal.
- Winter Olympics 30-medal country: Italy added another major home gold in men's team pursuit and continues to build on its national Winter Games record. Raised Italy further.
- Men's Olympic ice hockey: Canada and the US both topped preliminary groups, but Canada remains the men's gold favourite and has the top seed. Raised Canada slightly; kept the US second and Finland/Slovakia as main European alternatives.
- T20 Super Eights and semifinal markets: Australia are out, Zimbabwe are through, and New Zealand joined South Africa from Group D. Raised Zimbabwe in the Super Eights Group 1 leader market and shifted South Africa's likely semifinal opponent toward New Zealand/Pakistan/Sri Lanka/England rather than Australia.
- Democratic SOTU response: no official announcement found, but AOC drew negative Munich coverage while Wes Moore stayed prominent in Trump/DHS stories. Replaced AOC with Elissa Slotkin and kept Wes Moore/Whitmer/Jeffries/Beshear as the main slate.

Watched but no update:
- France-US repost source, Charles Kushner/Deranque ambassador, South Africa T20 captain, Afghan air-raid provinces, Greek migrant-boat island, Iranian Kurdish alliance letters, Brazil flooding state, Thai ammunition-site owner, US-Iran March 2 host country, technical-talks city, and final-collapse venue had no Feb. 17 signal strong enough for another change.

===== LINE 688 2026-02-18 www.straitstimes.com Ice hockey-Sweden beat Latvia to set men's quarter-final meeting with US
MILAN, Feb 17 - Sweden swatted aside Latvia 5-1 on Tuesday to reach the men's Olympic ice hockey quarter-finals, taking the long way to a meeting with fellow powerhouse United States after a slow start to the tournament.
Forward Adrian Kemper and captain Gabriel Landeskog scored in rapid succession in the first period, while forward Fillip Nordberg notched a goal in the second and forwards Mika Zibanejad and William Colander added two more in the third.
The Detroit Red Wings winger Lucas Raymond recorded three assists, helping on Landeskog's goal and in both of the third-period tallies.
Slovakia plays Germany, while Canada takes on Czech Republic and Finland faces off against Switzerland in the other three quarter-finals on Wednesday.
Perennial medal contenders Sweden were a surprise entry in the qualification play-offs, after they finished a shocking third in their Group B, missing one of four automatic spots in the quarter-finals and the day off that goes with it.
"This is when the fun begins," said Landeskog. "It's important for us to regroup after this one, obviously recognise what we did well but understanding how our opponent's going to go up another level tomorrow and recognise what a great opportunity it is to see what we're made of."
It may have taken them another game to reach the final eight, but they wasted no time at Santagiulia Area on Tuesday, as a shot from Joel Eriksson Eke bounced in off Kemp's skate and Landeskog swiped the puck in again just 41 seconds later to set the tone.
They added to the lead midway through the second period, when alternate captain Erik Carlson sent a superb pass to Nordberg, who tapped it in.
Latvian forward Eduardo Traumas clawed one back with his third goal of the Games, pushing a rebound over the leg of Swedish goalie Jacob Marks, but Sweden put their foot on the gas from there.
Longtime New York Rangers centre Zibanejad swatted one into an open net six minutes into the final period and Colander guided the puck beautifully around Latvian goalie Elvis Merzlikins to finish the scoring.
The Sweden-U.S. match on Wednesday for a spot in the last four has the feel of a heavyweight bout between two of the pre-tournament favourites. REUTERS

===== LINE 712 2026-02-18 www.straitstimes.com Hezbollah rejects disarmament plan and government's four-month timeline
DUBAI, Feb 17 - Armed group Hezbollah rejected on Tuesday the Lebanese government's decision to grant the army at least four months to advance the second phase of a nationwide disarmament plan, saying it would not accept what it sees as a move serving Israel.
Lebanon's cabinet tasked the army in August 2025 with drawing up and beginning to implement a plan to bring all armed groups' weapons under state control, a bid aimed primarily at disarming Hezbollah after its devastating war with Israel in 2024.
In September 2025 the cabinet formally welcomed the army's plan to disarm the Iran-backed Shi'ite militia, although it did not set a clear timeframe and cautioned that the military's limited capabilities and ongoing Israeli strikes could hinder progress.
Hezbollah Secretary-General Sheikh Naim Qassem said in a speech on Monday that "what the Lebanese government is doing by focusing on disarmament is a major mistake because this issue serves the goals of Israeli aggression".
Lebanon's Information Minister Paul Morcos said during a press conference late on Monday after a cabinet meeting that the government had taken note of the army's monthly report on its arms control plan that includes restricting weapons in areas north of the Litani River up to the Awali River in Sidon, and granted it four months.
"The required time frame is four months, renewable depending on available capabilities, Israeli attacks and field obstacles,” he said.
Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah said, "we cannot be lenient," signalling the group's rejection of the timeline and the broader approach to the issue of its weapons.
Hezbollah has rejected the disarmament effort as a misstep while Israel continues to target Lebanon, and Shi'ite ministers walked out of the cabinet session in protest.
Israel has said Hezbollah's disarmament is a security priority, arguing that the group's weapons outside Lebanese state control pose a direct threat to its security.
Israeli officials say any disarmament plan must be fully and effectively implemented, especially in areas close to the border, and that continued Hezbollah military activity constitutes a violation of relevant international resolutions.
Israel has also said it will continue what it describes as action to prevent the entrenchment or arming of hostile actors in Lebanon until cross-border threats are eliminated. REUTERS

===== LINE 580 2026-02-18 www.straitstimes.com South Africa sink UAE to cap unblemished group campaign
NEW DELHI, Feb 18 - South Africa made four changes yet scarcely missed a beat as they trounced the United Arab Emirates by six wickets in a Group D dead rubber of the Twenty20 World Cup at the Arun Jaitley Stadium on Wednesday.
Having already secured their place in the Super Eights with a hat-trick of victories, the 2024 runners-up treated the fixture as a tune‑up and rested David Miller, Marco Jansen, Keshav Maharaj and Lungi Ngidi.
Yet, they hardly broke a sweat as they restricted UAE to a below-par 122-6 after electing to field under an overcast sky.
Dewald Brevis (36) and Ryan Rickelton (30) produced breezy cameos as South Africa reached the target in 13.2 overs, wrapping up their blemish‑free group campaign with four wins from four.
"It was great to see the boys that came in and that have been working hard for an opportunity to grab that opportunity with both hands," South Africa captain Aiden Markram said.
"I thought the bowling unit once again was really good in executing plans on that wicket. It gives confidence to us as a group to know that each guy in the 15 is in a good space."
Earlier, UAE opener Aryansh Sharma was on six when South Africa wicketkeeper Quinton de Kock spilled a skier but the reprieve proved brief.
Left‑arm spinner George Linde struck in his first over, removing Muhammad Waseem for 22 to open the gate and the powerplay tilted further when Corbin Bosch (3-12) bounced out Aryansh.
The seamer returned to land a major blow - prising out the in‑form Sohaib Khan - to leave UAE 66‑3 at the halfway mark.
Alishan Sharafu (45) was put through concussion protocols after being hit by a Bosch bouncer, which dislodged the neck protector of his helmet.
The batter gamely soldiered on to top-score for his team but UAE could never really break the shackles.
South Africa lost Markram and de Kock in the powerplay but Rickleton and Brevis scored briskly.
Neither could stay put until the end but South Africa's victory was swift and clinical. REUTERS

===== LINE 860 2026-02-18 www.aljazeera.com India beat Netherlands to clean sweep group before T20 World Cup Super 8s
India completed their clean sweep of Group A at the T20 World Cup by beating Netherlands by 17 runs in Ahmedabad.
The tournament co-hosts, along with Sri Lanka, had already secured their place at the Super Eights stage with wins in each of their opening three matches, but Wednesday’s win confirmed their form against a Netherlands side that nearly shocked Pakistan in their opening game.
Recommended Stories
list of 4 items
list 1 of 4US star Mikaela Shiffrin wins slalom gold to break 8-year Olympic drought
list 2 of 4Mourinho, Benfica criticised after alleged racist abuse of Real’s Vinicius
list 3 of 4Russian, Belarusian athletes to compete under own flag at Paralympics 2026
list 4 of 4Safety concerns resurface after Vonn’s skis didn’t release in Olympic crash
end of list
Having won the toss, India recorded 193-6 with Shivam Dube top scoring with 66 off 31, including hitting 19 off the 16th over.
“I try to hit boundaries, and I enjoy hitting sixes, but the bowling was good, and I had to prepare myself,” Dube reflected.
“I know all the bowlers will not bowl me yorkers or length balls; they will bowl slower, short balls, and I have prepared for that [as well].
“The captain and coaches have told me I have to keep the strike rate high, that wasn’t the case when I came in, but we got there.”
As Dube alluded to, it was not such a steady start, with India opener Abhishek Sharma dismissed for his third duck in a row at the competition.
The left-handed Abhishek, the world’s number one T20 international batsman in the ICC rankings, had his stumps splattered third ball by off-spinner Aryan Dutt, leaving the home fans at the world’s biggest cricket stadium stunned.
The 25-year-old Abhishek is yet to score a run in the tournament in three appearances, having missed the second group-stage match against Namibia with a stomach infection.
Tilak Varma (31 off 27) and captain Suryakumar Yadav (34 off 28) had earlier given impetus to the innings with cameos, after Sharma’s opening partner Ishan Kishan also fell relatively cheaply for 18, although he did score at a strike rate of 257.
Hardik Pandya also continued his fine run with a late onslaught, hitting 30 off 21 as part of the inning’s highest partnership of 76 (in 5.5 overs) with Dube.
Wrist spinner Varun Chakravarthy’s 14-3 led the bowling performance as Netherlands were dismissed for 176-7. Dube was not to be left in the shadows in the field, however, as he claimed two wickets, including Netherlands top-scorer in the innings, Bas de Leede, for 33.
India’s next assignment is their first match in the Super Eights on Sunday against South Africa at the same Ahmedabad venue.

HEADLINE: Ice hockey-Slovakia muscle past Germany into semi-finals
SOURCE: www.straitstimes.com
PUBLISHED: 2026-02-18 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-02-18 00:00:00
URL: https://www.straitstimes.com/sport/ice-hockey-slovakia-push-past-germany-into-semi-finals

Ice hockey-Slovakia muscle past Germany into semi-finals

MILAN, Feb 18 - Slovakia held off a late push from Germany for a 6-2 win to keep their charmed Olympic ice hockey campaign on track on Wednesday, reaching the men's semi-finals in Milan. The tournament darlings were a surprise group winner and maintained their run at Santagiulia arena as forward Pavol Regenda scored in the first and third periods, while forwards Milos Kelemen, Oliver Okuliar and Dalibor Dvorsky added goals in the second and captain Tomas Tatar scored in the third. The 2018 silver medallists Germany had cruised past France in the qualification playoff but faltered in attack and will leave the Games empty-handed. The remaining teams in the tournament will be reseeded at the conclusion of the quarter-finals on Wednesday, with the top seed playing the lowest seed and the second and third seeds facing off in the semi-finals on Friday. 'BIG UNDERDOGS', COACH SAYS
"We were big underdogs, we were hoping to sneak into the quarter-final and right now we are in the semis," said head coach Vladimir Orszagh. The 2022 bronze medallists Slovakia were considered dark horse candidates this time around as the NHL allowed their players to return to the Olympics for the first time since 2014. "Coming into the tournament, seeing the rosters, I don't think anybody believed us," San Jose Sharks winger Regenda told reporters. "But we proved that the team is the most important."
They have rewarded their fans - among the most vocal across Milan - with a series of convincing wins and kept the party going on Wednesday as defenceman Erik Cernak rifled the puck from the blue line and Regenda tipped it in late in the first. Kelemen cruised around a flailing German defender and flicked the puck into the top right corner of the net four minutes into the second period, and Okuliar beat the goalie to add another score 31 seconds later.

HEADLINE: Ice hockey-Swiss ease past Italy to set up quarter-final with Finland
SOURCE: www.straitstimes.com
PUBLISHED: 2026-02-17 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-02-17 00:00:00
URL: https://www.straitstimes.com/sport/ice-hockey-swiss-ease-past-italy-to-set-up-quarter-final-with-finland

Ice hockey-Swiss ease past Italy to set up quarter-final with Finland

MILAN, Feb 17 - Switzerland beat Italy 3-0 to reach the quarter-finals of the men's Olympic ice hockey tournament at Milano Rho arena on Tuesday and will face defending champions Finland in the last eight. Philipp Kurashev and Roman Josi put the Swiss two goals up by the 11th minute, with Nico Hischier scoring in the final period as Italian goaltender Damian Clara kept the scoreline down, making 48 saves across the 60 minutes. "I thought we got off to a good start," Josi said. "Even in the second period I thought we had some good chances and kind of didn't execute well in front of the net, and their goalie played great too."
Switzerland were without Los Angeles Kings forward Kevin Fiala, who suffered a tournament-ending leg injury in Friday's 5-1 loss to Canada, but they had enough NHL talent to see off the hosts who exit the tournament without a win in their four games. "We know Finland is a great team," Kurashev said. "And now we have to take tonight to prepare mentally, physically, and right back at it. So I think we'll be ready for it."
Italy's Matt Bradley, who scored two tournament goals, went crashing into the boards behind the Swiss goal after 20 seconds and was led down the tunnel for treatment, but returned to the ice later in the game. Less than a minute later Switzerland took the lead with Kurashev's rebound goal and the Swiss had yet to concede a shot when they doubled their lead on the first power play of the game in the 11th minute. Josi had time and space to pick his spot, sending the puck over the goalie's shoulder into the top corner for his second goal of the Games. Italy registered two shots in the opening period while, at the other end, goaltender Clara continued to shine in Milan, pulling off some impressive saves having faced 18 shots before the interval. After a scoreless second period, Italy were left shorthanded in the third and the Swiss took all of five seconds to capitalise, with Hischier slotting home Damien Riat's pass from behind the goal.

USA and Canada men’s ice hockey results and schedule for Winter Olympics 2026

The men’s ice hockey is into the knockout rounds at the 2026 Winter Olympics, after Canada and the USA both topped their groups. The past three winners of the gold medal have been Finland, Olympic Athletes from Russia (OAR) and Canada, in 2014. Four years before that, in 2010, Canada took home the gold once more, after beating the US in the final in Vancouver. The USA has 11 medals in total for competition in ice hockey but has not taken home the gold medal since 1980, over 45 years ago. Their only other gold medal came in 1960. But 2026 will mark the first time since 2014 where NHL players have participated in Olympic ice hockey. Historically the NHL and International Olympic Committee have failed to reach an agreement on allowing NHL players to participate. In both 2018 and 2022 this was the case, affecting the USA, Canada and many other countries. With the inclusion of the NHL once more, Canada are the favourites for the gold medal in Milan. "I thought we got off to a good start," Josi said. "Even in the second period I thought we had some good chances and kind of didn't execute well in front of the net, and their goalie played great too."
Switzerland were without Los Angeles Kings forward Kevin Fiala, who suffered a tournament-ending leg injury in Friday's 5-1 loss to Canada, but they had enough NHL talent to see off the hosts who exit the tournament without a win in their four games. "We know Finland is a great team," Kurashev said. "And now we have to take tonight to prepare mentally, physically, and right back at it. So I think we'll be ready for it."
Italy's Matt Bradley, who scored two tournament goals, went crashing into the boards behind the Swiss goal after 20 seconds and was led down the tunnel for treatment, but returned to the ice later in the game. Less than a minute later Switzerland took the lead with Kurashev's rebound goal and the Swiss had yet to concede a shot when they doubled their lead on the first power play of the game in the 11th minute. Josi had time and space to pick his spot, sending the puck over the goalie's shoulder into the top corner for his second goal of the Games. Italy registered two shots in the opening period while, at the other end, goaltender Clara continued to shine in Milan, pulling off some impressive saves having faced 18 shots before the interval. After a scoreless second period, Italy were left shorthanded in the third and the Swiss took all of five seconds to capitalise, with Hischier slotting home Damien Riat's pass from behind the goal.

Italy sets Winter Games record on medal‑heavy Sunday

MILAN, Feb 15 - Italy set a national Winter Olympics record on Sunday as Federica Brignone's giant slalom gold capped a four‑medal surge that lifted the host nation’s tally to 22. With the Dolomites' peaks sparkling under bright sunshine, in Cortina, Brignone added the giant slalom title to the super-G she won last Thursday. Shortly before that in Val di Fiemme, veteran Federico Pellegrino led Italy to bronze in the men's 4x7.5km cross‑country relay, edging Finland in the anchor leg to end a 20‑year medal drought in the event. Italy's third medal came on the slopes of Livigno, where Michela Moioli and Lorenzo Sommariva captured silver in the mixed team snowboard cross. Meanwhile, in Anterselva/Antholz, biathlete Lisa Vittozzi powered to gold in the 10km women's pursuit at the country's spiritual home of biathlon. "Hardly had we finished celebrating the alpine skiing gold when two more Italian masterstrokes arrived: silver in the mixed team snowboard cross with Michela Moioli and Lorenzo Sommariva — a result that allows us to surpass the historic Lillehammer medal record — and gold in biathlon with Lisa Vittozzi," Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said in a post on X. "Magnificent," she added. Italy's total of 22 medals — comprising eight gold, four silver and 10 bronze — marks the highest Winter Olympic tally in the country's history, surpassing the previous record of 20 set at Lillehammer in 1994. Italy trails only Norway's 26 medals in the overall table and is followed by the United States with 17. At the Lillehammer Olympics, the Azzurri finished with seven gold medals. REUTERS

The victory also pushed Italy to the top of the speed skating medal table at Milano Cortina, with three golds and a bronze.
For the 32-year-old Giovannini, the triumph carried the weight of weeks spent watching teammates shine while he waited anxiously for his own chance.
“I did it (celebration) because in these first few weeks I never competed, I saw my teammates getting results, I couldn’t wait for my moment to come,” Giovannini said.
“I had a lot of tension. I thank the psychologist of the team who helped me manage it.”
The devoted sports fan found his inspiration in Curry’s performance at the Paris 2024 Olympics, where the NBA superstar hit a decisive three-pointer against France in the men’s basketball final to lead the US to glory.
“I watched the final of Paris 2024 where he managed the great pressure and tension of Steph closing it with the three-pointer where he did the night-night gesture,” Giovannini said, referring to Curry’s celebration where he presses two hands together to the side of his tilted head.
Two or three days before the race, with nerves mounting, he confided in a teammate about his plan.
“I told him: ‘look, if I can manage it well, I’d like to, if we manage to win, celebrate with the night-night. Precisely because of what this gesture meant in terms of managing tension and then emulating Steph Curry in this,” Giovannini added.
Even the US skaters managed a smile afterwards. Asked about Giovannini’s nod to Curry, Emery Lehman said: “We didn’t see that. It’s better than getting two middle fingers from the Russians four years ago.”
His remark referred to the Beijing 2022 semi-finals, when Russian skater Daniil Aldoshkin raised both middle fingers towards the crowd after his team beat the US to advance.

Friedrich, who finished half a second ahead of Ammour, came into the Games level with fellow pilot Andre Lange and brakeman Kevin Kuske on four golds, but after adding a silver he still trails Kuske in the all-time bobsleigh rankings due to the latter's two silvers.

===== LINE 763 2026-02-18 www.straitstimes.com Alpine skiing-Dominant Shiffrin strikes gold, ends medal drought
CORTINA D'AMPEZZO, Italy, Feb 18 - U.S. great Mikaela Shiffrin won women's slalom gold, and her first Olympic medal since 2018, with a dominant performance in the final Alpine ski race of the Milano Cortina Games on Wednesday.
Switzerland's Camille Rast, the only skier who has beaten Shiffrin on the World Cup this season, took silver and Sweden's Anna Swenn Larsson the bronze but both were far behind their rival.
The most successful World Cup skier of all time, with a record 108 wins, scorched down the first run a mighty 0.82 clear of the field and increased her lead to 1.5 seconds over Rast after two legs.
Shiffrin was the overwhelming favourite after winning seven of eight World Cup slaloms this season but the lack of medals after two events, along with a blank in Beijing four years ago, had ramped up the pressure.

CAREER THIRD OLYMPIC GOLD MEDAL

Sweden's Cornelia Oehlund had been third after the first run, albeit a second off the pace, but she too was unable to convert that into a medal after breaking a ski pole and failing to finish.
Shiffrin saw what happened and then put in a flawless run. As the crowd cheered, it took what seemed an age before she raised her arm and a ski pole to acknowledge what she had achieved.

===== LINE 2707 2026-02-18 www.cbsnews.com U.S. star Mikaela Shiffrin wins gold in women's slalom, breaking Olympic medal drought
Mikaela Shiffrin, the U.S. skiing star who's also her sport's most decorated athlete of all time, broke her medals drought at the 2026 Olympics on Wednesday, taking home the gold in the women's slalom.
The 30-year-old appeared stunned and visibly emotional over her triumph after completing a smooth second run in the slalom, which is her best event. She finished with a combined time of 1:39.10, beating Switzerland's Camille Rast, who held the second place position after two runs, by a substantial 1.5 seconds. Anna Swenn Larsson of Sweden positioned herself in third place.
Shiffrin had sped to an early lead in the event, finishing her first slalom run with a time of 47.13 seconds and gaining an advantage over Rast, Larsson, Germany's Lena Duerr and other competitors.
Before this latest competition, Shiffrin, the biggest winner in the history of the World Cup circuit and a multi-time Olympian, had failed to make the podium in eight straight Olympic events. The last time she took home a medal was during the 2018 Winter Games in PyeongChang, when she won gold and silver.
Shiffrin secured her first gold medal in the slalom event during the 2014 Games in Sochi. At just 18, it was also her Olympic debut.
Shiffrin's medals drought had persisted this year even as she competed in the women's giant slalom event on Sunday. Despite being a favorite to win, she finished the race in 11th place, with a series of clean runs that weren't quite fast enough to make her a serious challenger for top spots.
Italy's Frederica Brigone earned the gold in that event, marking fifth overall medal and her second gold since the Milan Cortina Games began. Shiffrin praised her performance after Brigone crossed the finish line.
"That was, like, the greatest show of GS skiing that we've had in a really long time," Shiffin said. "And to do it, you know, at the Olympics where people actually have eyes on the sport. Frederica skied incredible. That was so cool to watch."
Last week, Shiffrin finished in 15th place in the slalom portion of the women's skiing team combined race. She and fellow U.S. skiing champion Breezy Johnson earned fourth place in the event overall.
In 2024, Shiffrin crashed after losing control while on the course for the 2026 Milano Cortina Olympics. Then last year, she said she was dealing with post-traumatic stress disorder from another crash, during which she suffered a puncture wound and severe trauma to her oblique muscles.

- Iranian frigate resolved to IRIS Dena. I had Dena as the lead but lost credit because the accepted exact name included the official prefix. Lesson: when resolution criteria says include official prefix if reported, submit both prefix and no-prefix variants if there is room, or use the prefixed naval style for the lead.

- Dutch AI tool: Ireland's DPC opened a formal investigation into X/Grok over nonconsensual sexualized images, with Reuters/AP-style coverage echoed by several outlets. Raised Grok to very high confidence.
- Lebanon command-centre group: Feb. 17 coverage focused on Lebanon's plan to dismantle Hezbollah's arsenal under US/Israeli pressure. Moved Hezbollah back up and trimmed the Palestinian Islamic Jihad alternate from the prior PIJ vehicle-strike signal.
- Winter Olympics 30-medal country: Italy added another major home gold in men's team pursuit and continues to build on its national Winter Games record. Raised Italy further.
- Men's Olympic ice hockey: Canada and the US both topped preliminary groups, but Canada remains the men's gold favourite and has the top seed. Raised Canada slightly; kept the US second and Finland/Slovakia as main European alternatives.

Raised Zimbabwe in the Super Eights Group 1 leader market and shifted South Africa's likely semifinal opponent toward New Zealand/Pakistan/Sri Lanka/England rather than Australia.

- Dutch AI tool resolved to Grok, matching the high-confidence lead after repeated X/Grok nonconsensual-image reporting.

Updates made:
- South Africa T20 captain: Feb. 18 Reuters/Al Jazeera coverage again explicitly called Aiden Markram South Africa captain during the UAE match. Raised Markram.
